title: Hieracium spanocomum
intro: Hieracium spanocomum, also known as hawkweed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing and pain relief. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium spanocomum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium spanocomum is commonly prepared as a tincture or decoction to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Hieracium spanocomum is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The genus Hieracium is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds, which are native to various parts of the world. Hieracium spanocomum is further classified as a species within the genus Hieracium. Its classification also places it within the kingdom Plantae.
